records.ety: [Probably for older tolter; cf. AS. tealtrian to totter, vacillate. Cf.Tilt to incline, Toddle, Tottle, Totty.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Tottered (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Tottering.]

defs: To shake so as to threaten a fall; to vacillate; to be unsteady; to stagger; as, an old man totters with age.
To shake; to reel; to lean; to waver.
quotes: Troy nods from high, and totters to her fall.
